items.headline: Four garage-to-JADU conversions permitted in same week
items.summary: San José issued permits for converting garages into JADUs (Junior Accessory Dwelling Units) at four separate residential properties within three days. The cluster suggests homeowners are taking advantage of the city's JADU program to add rental units while retaining garage space.

items.headline: Parks and Recreation Strategic Plan adopted
items.summary: City Council adopted a strategic plan governing parks and recreation services. The plan sets direction for how the city will manage and develop its park system and recreation programs.

items.headline: City presents five-year decarbonization plan and goals
items.summary: During a study session, City Council heard the city's decarbonization goal and a five-year approach to achieving it. The plan outlines specific actions Mountain View will take to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.

items.headline: City approves $1.6M agreement with Highway Patrol for FIFA World Cup traffic
items.summary: Santa Clara City Council and Stadium Authority Board approved a standard agreement with the California Highway Patrol for traffic management services during the FIFA World Cup, with a projected cost of $1.6 million. The council also approved the related budget amendment to cover the expense.

items.headline: Equinix data center receives $138M building permit
items.summary: A new data center facility is cleared to build at 131 Great Oaks Boulevard.

items.headline: Congregation Kol Emeth permitted to add glass enclosure to trellis
items.summary: The synagogue at 4175 Manuela Avenue received approval to partially enclose an existing trellis with glass, increasing the building's lot coverage. The project will modify the congregation's outdoor space.

items.headline: City agrees to Seeds of Peace Monument artwork at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: San José City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust to install a public artwork titled Seeds of Peace by artist Mario Chiodo at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The monument will be a permanent public art installation at the riverside venue.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District sets FY 2026-27 budget
items.summary: The council took action on the 2026-2027 budget and annual assessment for the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District, which funds improvements and services in that commercial area.
